MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ingrid M. Evans, Esq. of the Evans Law Firm, Tel. 415.441.8669, for Plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ERNEST CHIN'S Motion For Protective Order (Amended) is Denied with respect to the depositions of Jong and Low. While sympathetic to Plaintiffs' representations that they are elderly and frail, and may experience stress in connection with being deposed, these are not sufficient reasons to block the discovery sought. Assuming that the mediation set for September 21, 2012 is unsuccessful, the depositions of Jong and Low shall take place no later than October 26, 2012, and each shall last for no more than seven hours (and it is hoped the length would be shorter). It is the understanding of the Court that the request to depose Lucille Chin is withdrawn. Granted with respect to the documents, which appear covered by the patient-psychiatrist privilege, which survives the death of the patient. The mental state of the decedent Chin is not directly at stake in this case, the parties already have some evidence of his pertinent state (dementia, etc.), and it is a matter of speculation that the records would contain additional materials useful to the parties. On balance, the motion in this respect should be granted. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
